commit.message:
fix: sync `class_names` from dataset after training so `predict()` returns correct labels (#816)

* sync class_names from dataset after training so predict() returns correct labels (#509)

After trainer.fit(), RFDETR.train() now syncs RFDETRDataModule.class_names
back to self.model.class_names so that predict() returns dataset labels
instead of falling through to COCO defaults.

BestModelCallback._save_checkpoint() now enriches TrainConfig with dataset
class names (regular and EMA paths) before saving, so reloaded checkpoints
also carry the correct label mapping.

Five regression tests added covering both the in-memory sync and the
checkpoint serialisation paths.

- Updated `class_names` checks to use `is None` instead of truthiness in multiple paths (e.g., training sync, checkpoint saving) to avoid overwriting empty lists.
- Added regression tests validating that explicit `class_names=[]` are not replaced by dataset defaults (regular and EMA checkpoint paths).
- Use `getattr` for `config` and `datamodule` to avoid potential attribute errors during `class_names` sync.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(looplab): add fluid preset seam validation

Summary:
- add a graphics-backed fluid preset validator that checks loop closure,
  preview closure, edge continuity, and motion across 2s, 3s, and 4s
- write fluid validation snapshots to log/fluid-validation for inspection
- run the new validator from the end-to-end batch validation flow

Rationale:
- protect the fluid preset against regressions that reintroduce visible
  tiling or break the loop boundary contract
- keep fluid coverage aligned with the existing landscape and geometric
  validation passes

Tests:
- "/Applications/Unity/Hub/Editor/6000.3.7f1/Unity.app/Contents/MacOS/Unity"
  -batchmode -projectPath /tmp/loop-lab-fluid-verify.SbUKVo -quit
  -logFile /tmp/loop-lab-fluid-verify.SbUKVo/log/fluid-validator-rerun2.log
  -executeMethod Precondition.LoopLab.Editor.LoopLabFluidPresetValidator.Run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(looplab): enforce exact shader loop closure

Summary:
- route preview rendering through normalized phase and RenderAtPhase
- add shared loop-safe HLSL helpers and refactor shipped shaders to
  use closed-orbit motion
- validate boundary closure across multiple seeds and add loop
  contract edit-mode tests

Rationale:
- keep preview, export, and boundary QA on the same phase contract so
  frame 0 matches phase 1.0 exactly
- prevent regressions from Unity time globals and duplicate end-frame
  exports as new presets evolve

Tests:
- git diff --check
- rg scan for banned Unity time globals in shipped shaders
- Unity compile/tests not run: batchmode was blocked by another open
  Unity instance and the editor Action Host connection was refused

feat: add /research skill — autonomous goal-directed iteration loop

- Add `.claude/skills/research/SKILL.md`: metric-driven iteration with atomic commits, auto-rollback on regression, specialist agent ideation (perf-optimizer, sw-engineer, ai-researcher), team mode for parallel axis exploration, Colab MCP for GPU workloads (opt-in)
- Add `.mcp.json`: colab-mcp server definition (inactive unless enabled in settings.local.json)
- Fix statusline: 3 always-visible lines — agents (🕵), codex (🤖), tools (🔧); 🤖 codex ×N when active, none when idle; removed () from tools line
- Fix task-log.js: add `m` flag to regex so multi-line Bash scripts with `codex exec` are tracked
- Add `Bash(git commit:*)` and `Bash(git revert:*)` to settings.json + permissions-guide.md
- Audit fix-all: replace stale version pins with <CURRENT> placeholders in linting-expert; replace low-value comment-only blocks with prose bullets in perf-optimizer, qa-specialist, sw-engineer; renumber optimize steps (2b→3, 3→4, 4→5); fix Colab MCP setup instructions (settings.local.json, not .mcp.json)
- README: add /research to skills table + usage examples; add Colab MCP opt-in note
- review/SKILL.md: indent Recommended Next Steps list items

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(bus): bound redundant timeout detail logging

Summary:
- emit redundant exchange-timeout detail as bounded telemetry and
  add it to the canonical public event list
- rate-limit repeated identical partial-arrival timeout warnings and
  log once when the degraded timeout pattern clears
- cover the new telemetry emitter and the end-to-end redundant-bus
  logging behavior with focused tests

Rationale:
- per-cycle partial-arrival warnings flood operator logs on degraded
  redundant links even though the detailed signal is already useful as
  machine-readable telemetry
- the runtime should keep high-signal state-transition logs while
  moving repeated exchange detail out of the warning path

Validation:
- mix test test/telemetry_test.exs test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(capabilities): tighten actionable operator guidance

Summary:
- stop queueing continuation drafts unless the target thread actually opens
- filter workflow runbooks to ready workflows and skip no-op starter-pack activation recommendations
- add focused backend and cockpit regression coverage for the reviewed cases

Rationale:
- prevent the new Continue flow from leaking follow-up text into the wrong active thread
- keep operator guidance and runbooks aligned with what can actually run under current policy and workflow state

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_workflows.py
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill runtime docs hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased pending changelog hashes for the runtime
  hardening and inline public docs work with the real short SHA

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy changes to carry their real
  commit hash before handoff

Validation:
- not rerun; changelog-only follow-up

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(api): harden runtime edges and inline public docs

Summary:
- roll back partially started DC and domain runtime on activation
  failure and preserve the rolled-back state in master activation
- reject unknown slave signal and latch subscriptions instead of
  accepting them silently
- make domain image access use explicit ETS lookup, extract small
  shared link and session helpers, and move public runtime docs
  inline with function-level API documentation

Rationale:
- failed activation must not leak partially running runtime pieces
  behind a master that still reports pre-activation state
- subscription typos should fail fast, and domain hot-path access
  should avoid exception-driven control flow
- keeping public docs on the modules themselves makes the API easier
  to discover and align with the current implementation

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix docs --warnings-as-errors --formatter html
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: adjust det. head reinitialization logic for class count mismatches (#815)

- Refined logic to reinitialize detection head only when the checkpoint has more classes than the model configuration.
- Added warning for scenarios when the checkpoint has fewer classes than configured, retaining checkpoint class count.
- Centralized warning logic for class count mismatches in `validate_checkpoint_compatibility`.
- Removed duplicate warnings from training module and DETR methods.
- Added unit tests in `test_state_dict.py` to ensure warning behavior correctness.
- Adjusted monkeypatch target for `os.path.isfile` to module-specific path for improved isolation.
- Preserved and restored the logger's original `propagate` state in tests to ensure proper cleanup.
- Consolidated logic to reinitialize detection head only when checkpoint class count exceeds configured task-specific classes.
- Removed redundant code paths for handling mismatches.
- Adjusted logic to ensure fine-tuned checkpoint weights are preserved when loading fewer checkpoint classes than the model's default configuration.
- Added safeguards for explicit user-defined `num_classes` to re-expand detection head after checkpoint load.
- Updated regression tests to reflect expanded scenarios, including user overrides and auto-alignment cases.
- Cleaned up outdated references to issue #813 in docstrings.

---------

Summary:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(recovery): rediscover anonymous returning slaves

Summary:
- fall back to topology rediscovery during recovery when a PDO slave
  returns without its old fixed station address
- keep the bus alive during runtime-only teardown and reset master
  state for a fresh discovery pass
- make simulator power cycles clear fixed station identity and add
  regression coverage for anonymous return and reconnect retry races

Rationale:
- real hardware can reboot anonymous after a disconnect, so FPRD on
  the old station never recovers even though the topology is visible
  again
- simulator disconnect and power-cycle behavior now matches that
  hardware failure mode closely enough to keep recovery coverage
  realistic

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(api): allow loopback cockpit origin

Summary:
- allow 127.0.0.1 dev origins through backend CORS so the cockpit can call the new capability endpoints from the local frontend
- add a regression test proving the capability overview preflight succeeds for the loopback browser origin

Rationale:
- the local frontend was serving from http://127.0.0.1:3000 while the backend only allowed localhost origins
- that mismatch made the operator surface fall back to unknown and 0/0 state even though the new capability APIs were healthy

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_app.py tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_workflows.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(recovery): retry down-slave reconnect healing

Summary:
- retry reconnect authorization for tracked slave :down faults in the
  recovering loop instead of depending on a one-shot reconnect event
- treat steady-state slave :down faults as retryable so noncritical
  slaves also reheal once wire access returns
- document Bus.Link.Submission as internal rather than hidden so public
  specs no longer point at a hidden module type
- add focused recovery tests and note the reconnect-healing fix in the
  changelog

Rationale:
- a slave that physically came back could remain stuck in :down if the
  first reconnect authorization window was missed or raced
- the master already owns re-entry, so it should keep retrying that
  authorization while the slave remains tracked as down
- generated docs should not expose types from a hidden nested module

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master_recovery_bus_test.exs test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test (aborted by user during full-suite rerun)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(validation): clarify fresh-checkout blockers (#14)

Summary:
- refresh the final validation report and linked docs so they separate
  the validated repo rerun from the still-blocked user-rig lane on a
  fresh checkout
- record the clean rerun results from current main and remove the
  stale step-count wording in the report
- extend the doc contract test to lock the validated rerun verdict and
  blocker language into the checked-in report

Rationale:
- the merged HEL-49 report still described a previously prepared HEL-54
  host, which no longer matched what a new engineer sees from a clean
  checkout when local-only inputs and native deps are absent
- tightening that wording keeps the repo handoff accurate and makes the
  remaining risk explicit without depending on branch lore

Tests:
- make test
- make check
- ./scripts/fetch_orbslam3_baseline.sh
- make monocular-prereqs (expected non-zero: local-only inputs and
  native toolchain still missing on a fresh checkout)

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(monocular): codify local prereq bootstraps (#13)

Summary:
- add repo-local OpenCV and Boost bootstrap scripts plus Make targets
- teach the prereq checker and build wrapper to reuse local OpenCV,
  Boost, and Pangolin prefixes
- update the operator docs and final validation report to the
  HEL-52 dataset contract and the HEL-54 Pangolin blocker
- extend tooling and prereq tests for the new local bootstrap paths

Rationale:
- HEL-54 recovered the lens-10 private bundle and proved the native
  build can progress through DBoW2, g2o, and Sophus once local

  reproducible instead of leaving it as shell history

Summary:
- surface redundant-port send failures in runtime topology, fault state,
  and link-health telemetry
- default slave AL health polling to 250ms and fail writes fast when
  a slave is already marked down
- add simulator power-cycle faults that clear volatile mailbox and
  process-data mapping state before reset

- degraded redundancy and full disconnects were previously too easy to
  miss in runtime state on real and simulated hardware

  than preserve mailbox transfers or injected mailbox faults
- keeping the changelog and local ignore rules in sync avoids carrying

commit.message:
Fix idle app-server port messages in AgentRunner (#52) closes #51 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | `AgentRunner` started a persistent app-server session and then returned from `init/1` while the GenServer still owned the port. | The same startup path runs, but idle port traffic is handled explicitly by `AgentRunner.handle_info/2`. | | Observable effect | Late startup notifications such as `{"method":"thread/started"...}` hit the GenServer mailbox and produced `received unexpected message in handle_info/2` error logs on normal session startup. | The same late notifications are drained as known idle app-server traffic, producing no unexpected-message error while the session remains usable for later dispatches. | | Affected inputs | Persistent-session runs where the Codex app-server emits JSON-RPC notifications after `AppServer.start_session/1` returns. | The same persistent-session runs, plus idle app-server exit status handling between dispatches. | ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant AR as AgentRunner GenServer participant AS as Codex AppServer Port AR->>AS: start_session / thread/start AS-->>AR: thread/start response AS-->>AR: thread/started notification Note over AR: Before: unexpected handle_info error Note over AR: After: explicitly ignored idle notification AR->>AS: later turn/start AS-->>AR: turn/completed ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: Normal turn execution still flows through `AppServer.run_turn/4` in the same process, and persistent-session reuse continues to work across dispatches. - **Explicitly unchanged**: The JSON-RPC protocol handling inside `AppServer` during active request/turn loops is unchanged; this PR only covers messages that arrive while the GenServer is idle. - **Out of scope**: No changes to orchestrator issue routing, workpad handling, or Codex protocol semantics beyond suppressing/handling idle port traffic. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Late startup notification still leaks as an unexpected GenServer message | Added regression `persistent agent runner ignores idle app-server startup notifications` and verified direct before/after harness output. | N/A (local command) | | Persistent-session reuse regresses while adding idle-port handling | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs:2065` passed alongside the new regression. | N/A (local command) | | Broader Elixir quality gate regresses | `mix test`, `mix setup`, and `make -C elixir all` all passed locally. | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Idle app-server messages could be over-filtered or an idle port exit could stop a runner sooner than expected. - **Blast radius**: `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner` persistent-session lifecycle and the focused persistent-session test coverage in `core_test.exs`. - **How to detect**: Watch for new persistent-session startup errors, unexpected runner termination between dispatches, or failures in the new idle-port regression test. - **How to rollback**: Revert commit `5485797` to restore the prior `AgentRunner` behavior. ### Review Focus 1. Confirm the new `AgentRunner.handle_info/2` clauses only consume app-server traffic while the runner is idle and do not interfere with normal turn processing. 2. Check whether stopping the runner on idle `{:exit_status, status}` is the right failure mode for a dead persistent app-server port. 3. Verify the regression test matches the production symptom by emitting `thread/started` immediately after the `thread/start` response.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change. This is an internal orchestrator/app-server integration fix with no external API or UI surface change. ### Container (C4-L2) No container topology change. The same Elixir application process still owns the local Codex app-server stdio port. ### Component (C4-L3) `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner` now owns both persistent-session dispatch and idle port draining responsibilities, while `SymphonyElixir.Codex.AppServer` continues to own active request/turn protocol handling. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner.State` -> idle line buffer added for owned port traffic - `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner.handle_info/2` -> explicit idle port `:data` / `:exit_status` handling - `SymphonyElixir.CoreTest` -> regression covering late `thread/started` notification after startup ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Handle idle persistent-session port traffic in `AgentRunner` instead of changing `AppServer`'s synchronous receive loops. - **Alternatives considered**: Add more generic asynchronous protocol handling to `AppServer`, or ignore all unexpected GenServer messages with a catch-all `handle_info/2`. - **Trade-offs**: This keeps the fix narrow and local to the owning GenServer, but it adds a small amount of port parsing logic to `AgentRunner`. - **Why chosen**: The bug only appears after `AgentRunner` becomes the long-lived port owner, so handling the idle mailbox there fixes the production symptom without widening the `AppServer` protocol surface. - **Implementation links**: Local diff in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/agent_runner.ex` and `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs` on commit `5485797`.
Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
close cancel-before-progress race in processing lifecycle

Cancelling immediately after clicking Process showed a green "Processing
completed" status instead of the gray "... canceled by user". 3 related fixes:

1. ProgressManager inferred cancellation exclusively from progress bar
   errorState. When cancel fires before backend progress arrives, bars are
   empty, causing a fallback to the success branch. Cancellation is now an
   explicit prop passed from App.svelte, independent of bar existence.

2. Race window between ProcessingService.isProcessing and GUIHandler
   cancelFunc initialization caused early cancels to drop. GUIHandler now
   buffers pending cancels similar to ProcessingService (commit 45fd459).

3. GUIHandler.SendProcessingRequest incorrectly returned nil, forcing a
   "success" status for cancelled jobs. The handler now returns
   processCtx.Err(), allowing the completion event to correctly differentiate
   between "cancelled", "error", and "success".

Note: The throttler event ordering issue (stale progress.batch after
progress.reset) was deemed theoretical & not observed in practice; left as-is.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(sip): reuse invite cseq for ack

Fix the SIP ACK generation so it reuses the INVITE CSeq number, which is required for 2xx ACKs in SIP dialogs. This prevents Yeastar/Asterisk from dropping the call because of a malformed ACK.

Add a regression test that asserts the ACK is emitted with the previous INVITE CSeq.

commit.message:
Phase 7: add owner-aware epoch rewards

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep live Codex sessions across review re-entry (#46) closes #44 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | Each orchestrator dispatch started a fresh `AgentRunner` task, `AppServer.start_session/1` always created a new Codex thread, and `agent_runner` closed the app-server port at the end of every run. | The orchestrator now keeps a persistent per-issue `AgentRunner` alive across non-terminal dispatch boundaries, reuses its existing app-server session for later turns, and only tears it down during terminal cleanup or after an unexpected runner crash. | | Observable effect | `status:review -> status:in-progress` reused only the logical `issue_session_id`; the physical `thread_id` was rebuilt from scratch and review churn kept paying the full context rebuild cost. | Re-entry and continuation dispatches on the same live issue reuse the same physical Codex session/thread, session stats/dashboard keep logical vs physical identifiers separate, and dispatches fall back to a fresh physical session only when no live runner remains. | | Affected inputs | Review re-entry, retry, and stall recovery all depended on transition-specific session-reuse branching and the previous physical thread could not survive port teardown. | Any dispatch targeting an issue with a live runner uses the existing session; issues without a live runner still start fresh deterministically, and terminal issue cleanup releases the long-lived app-server session. | ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant O as Orchestrator participant R as Persistent AgentRunner participant C as Codex app-server O->>R: first in-progress dispatch R->>C: start_session (once) C-->>R: thread_id O->>R: later review/continuation dispatch R->>C: turn/start on existing thread C-->>R: reused physical session O->>R: done / terminal cleanup R->>C: stop_session ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: logical `issue_session_id` continuity, retry/stall recovery semantics, and deterministic fresh-session startup when no live runner exists. - **Explicitly unchanged**: workspace provisioning, issue-session accounting, and the overall session stats/dashboard surfaces remain in the same stores and presenters. - **Out of scope**: cross-worker migration of a live app-server session and any redesign of unrelated backoff/retry policy beyond keeping the runner alive long enough to reuse the same session locally. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Persistent runners could lose physical-session reuse across dispatches | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/app_server_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/session_stats_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | Long-lived runner lifecycle could regress the wider Elixir app | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | Dashboard/session stats could blur logical vs physical session identity | Snapshot/presenter/session-stats updates in this branch plus the targeted test suite above | N/A (repo files + local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: the orchestrator could leave a runner marked busy/idle incorrectly, causing slot accounting or cleanup bugs after a persistent session completes or crashes. - **Blast radius**: `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator`, `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner`, `SymphonyElixir.Codex.AppServer`, session stats/presenter/dashboard surfaces, and the focused tests/docs around session reuse. - **How to detect**: failing `orchestrator_status`, `core`, `app_server`, `session_stats`, or snapshot tests; runtime logs showing duplicate `thread/start` calls for the same live issue or leaked runners after terminal cleanup. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47` to restore per-dispatch runner teardown. ### Review Focus 1. Confirm the orchestrator now owns long-lived runner lifecycle and can redispatch into an idle live runner instead of spawning a fresh task. 2. Confirm `AgentRunner` keeps the same app-server session/thread across dispatches and only marks reuse when a live session actually exists. 3. Confirm session stats/dashboard output still distinguishes logical and physical identifiers while handling fresh-session fallback cleanly after runner loss.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 service boundary changed. This PR changes the internal lifecycle of the orchestrator-managed Codex runtime so repeated issue dispatches can reuse an existing live physical session instead of rebuilding context every time. ### Container (C4-L2) The same Elixir orchestrator container still launches the Codex app-server process, but the process now lives under a persistent per-issue runner instead of a one-dispatch task. Session stats and the dashboard continue to read from the same in-process stores. ### Component (C4-L3) ```mermaid flowchart LR O[Orchestrator] --> R[Persistent AgentRunner] R --> A[Codex.AppServer session] O --> S[SessionStats] S --> P[Presenter / Dashboard] ``` The orchestrator tracks a live runner per issue, dispatches more turns into that runner while the issue stays active, `AgentRunner` keeps the Codex app-server session alive, and session stats/presenter/dashboard continue surfacing both logical and physical session identifiers. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - Orchestrator live-runner dispatch/reuse lifecycle -> https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex - Persistent runner start/dispatch/stop path -> https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/agent_runner.ex - App-server session reuse marker -> https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/app_server.ex - Session reuse docs and observability surfaces -> https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/docs/session_reuse.md ### Decision Record - **Decision**: keep a live per-issue runner/app-server session until terminal cleanup instead of trying to reconnect to a dead physical thread after each dispatch. - **Alternatives considered**: continue the logical-only stopgap, or keep the old resume/fallback path that attempted to reconnect to a persisted thread id after app-server teardown. - **Trade-offs**: this adds long-lived runner lifecycle/state management in the orchestrator, but removes dead resume logic and makes physical session reuse real for repeated dispatches on the same issue. - **Why chosen**: Codex threads die when the app-server port is closed, so true physical reuse requires keeping the owning runner/session alive across dispatch boundaries. - **Implementation links**: https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex ; https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/agent_runner.ex ; https://github.com/Stevengre/siaan/blob/f2da581d3458048514c22d6972eec3240a350b47/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/app_server.ex
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
Phase 7: Haskell-align follower epoch state and snapshot replay

Follower-side ledger state is now materially closer to the Haskell reference.

Highlights:
- hydrate reward, deposit, current/future pool, fee-pot, and snapshot stake state from Mithril ancillary payloads
- stage pool re-registration into future pool params and activate them at epoch processing
- keep pool reward accounts inside mark/set/go snapshots so epoch rewards use snapshot-era pool accounts
- tighten certificate and withdrawal handling around tracked local state
- update docs to reflect the verified bootstrap/runtime follower path

Validated with the local unit suite, origin transition test, and live preprod sync/bootstrap-sync runs.

commit.message:
feat(guardian): deepen outcome learning signals

Summary:
- extend guardian learning signals so repeated positive and acknowledged outcomes can influence direct-delivery timing and native-channel preference
- wire the richer signal into intervention policy, proactive delivery, tests, and the deterministic guardian outcome-learning eval
- update the implementation docs so guardian-learning-loop-v2 is marked shipped on this branch and the queue advances to cross-surface continuity

Rationale:
- Seraph was only learning one narrow lesson from outcomes: reduce interruptions after negative feedback
- the next high-value step is to let outcomes also inform when a high-salience nudge should cut through and when native notification fallback is the better channel

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_guardian_feedback.py tests/test_intervention_policy.py tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario guardian_outcome_learning --indent 2
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: seraph
repository.description: AI agent with a retro 16-bit RPG village UI — Phaser 3 canvas, persistent identity, long-term memory, hierarchical goals, and plug-and-play MCP server integration

commit.message:
feat(cockpit): add workflow run operator views

Summary:
- enrich workflow audit results with step tools, artifact paths, and degraded-step metadata
- add dedicated cockpit workflow-run views with workflow-specific inspector actions and linked artifact lineage
- advance the implementation docs so cockpit-workflow-views-v1 is marked shipped on this branch

Rationale:
- workflow composition existed, but the cockpit still flattened runs into generic audit rows and made operator control harder than it should be
- exposing structured workflow-run evidence gives the cockpit a stable surface for review, replay, and artifact-aware navigation without adding a whole new backend subsystem yet

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_tool_audit.py tests/test_workflows.py
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/inspector.test.ts src/components/cockpit/layouts.test.ts src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(trust): harden workflow secret containment

Summary:
- restrict secret reference resolution to explicit injection-safe tool surfaces
- reject workflows whose runtime step tools are underdeclared and expose secret-ref acceptance in tool and workflow metadata
- update runtime evals and implementation docs so execution-safety-hardening-v2 is marked shipped on this branch

Rationale:
- prevent secret refs from flowing through generic wrapped tools that were never meant to receive them
- keep workflow risk, approval, and execution-boundary metadata aligned with actual runtime step tools instead of optimistic declarations

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_secret_ref_tools.py tests/test_tools_api.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario tool_policy_guardrails_behavior --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
